items.quote: Bahrain International has streamlined our training pipelines. Our student cohort completion rate improved by 42% after introducing interactive 3DVista integrations.
items.author: Dr. Ahmad R., Director of Education

items.quote: The multi-tenant architecture allowed our travel agency to deploy a custom-branded portal in under an hour. Outstanding security and localization infrastructure.
items.author: Sarah W., Head of Operations

items.quote: RTL support for Arabic paired with dynamic quiz rule validation has made this the definitive platform for our international corporate training initiatives.
items.author: Hassan Al-M., Training Manager

items.q: Does Bahrain International render 360° panoramas directly?
items.a: No. The system delegates content creation to 3DVista. You build and export your tours using 3DVista, and upload the static zip output to our CMS. Bahrain hosts, serves, and tracks access to the tour as a learning step.

items.q: How does the Learning Rule Engine operate?
items.a: You can define logical triggers via the CMS (e.g., user must finish the orientation video, interact with the virtual tour, and achieve an 80% score on the quiz before unlocking the next module).

items.q: Does the platform support dynamic domain white-labeling?
items.a: Yes. Each tenant can map their own custom domain or subdomain, customize application colors, upload logos, and apply distinct certificate layout designs.

my_learning_wizard.lesson_1_1_content:
## Introduction
Performing Hajj is not only a spiritual journey but also a physical one.
Pilgrims will spend long periods walking, standing, and moving between holy sites under hot weather conditions.
Proper physical preparation helps reduce fatigue, lowers health risks, and allows pilgrims to perform every ritual more comfortably and safely.
Preparing your body several weeks before departure is one of the best investments you can make for a successful pilgrimage.

## Why Physical Preparation Matters
Many pilgrims underestimate the physical demands of Hajj.
During the pilgrimage, you may:
• Walk several kilometers each day.
• Stand for long periods during rituals.
• Travel between multiple holy locations.
• Experience high temperatures.
• Face crowded environments.
Without proper preparation, these activities may become physically exhausting.
Good physical fitness allows pilgrims to focus more on worship rather than physical discomfort.

## Recommended Physical Activities
Begin preparing your body at least 4-8 weeks before departure.
Recommended exercises include:
• Walking 30-45 minutes every day.
• Climbing stairs to strengthen leg muscles.
• Light stretching every morning and evening.
• Simple breathing exercises.
• Low-impact cardio exercises.
Exercise gradually. Do not force yourself beyond your physical ability. Consult your physician if you have existing medical conditions.

## Medical Preparation
Before traveling, make sure you have completed all required medical preparations.
Checklist:
✓ Medical examination.
✓ Mandatory vaccinations.
✓ Prescription medications.
✓ Personal medical records.
✓ Health insurance documents.
✓ Emergency contact information.
If you have chronic illnesses such as hypertension, diabetes, asthma, or heart disease, consult your doctor before departure.

## Personal Equipment
Prepare equipment that supports physical comfort during worship.
Recommended items:
• Comfortable walking shoes.
• Extra sandals.
• Moisture-absorbing socks.
• Water bottle.
• Small backpack.
• Umbrella.
• Hat.
• Sunscreen.
• Personal medications.

## Maintaining Your Health During Hajj
While in Saudi Arabia:
• Drink water regularly.
• Avoid skipping meals.
• Get enough rest.
• Wear comfortable footwear.
• Follow guidance from your group leader.
• Avoid unnecessary physical activity outside worship schedules.
If you feel unwell, immediately report your condition to healthcare officers.

## Best Practices
To improve your physical readiness:
✔ Exercise consistently.
✔ Sleep 7-8 hours daily.
✔ Eat balanced meals.
✔ Maintain hydration.
✔ Practice walking using your pilgrimage footwear.
✔ Build stamina gradually.

## Common Mistakes
Avoid these mistakes:
❌ Starting physical training only a few days before departure.
❌ Ignoring medical advice.
❌ Wearing new shoes during Hajj.
❌ Forgetting personal medication.
❌ Not drinking enough water.
❌ Overexerting yourself before the pilgrimage begins.

## Key Takeaways
Remember these important points:
• Hajj requires physical endurance.
• Begin exercising several weeks before departure.
• Complete all medical requirements.
• Prepare proper equipment.
• Maintain healthy habits throughout the pilgrimage.

## Reflection
Take a moment to reflect.
Have you already begun preparing physically for Hajj?
Which preparations have you completed?
What areas still need improvement before departure?

## Next Learning Activity
After completing this reading material, continue with:
▶ Video
The next lesson will demonstrate practical examples of physical preparation before Hajj.
Click "Next: Watch Video" to continue.
my_learning_wizard.lesson_1_2_content:
## Understanding Miqat Boundaries

### What is Miqat?
Miqat is the boundary place or region determined in Islamic law as the starting location for the intention of ihram for hajj and umrah pilgrims. Every pilgrim entering Makkah for mandatory worship must wear the ihram clothes and make the ihram intention before crossing the Miqat boundary appropriate to their arrival route.

By understanding the location of Miqat, pilgrims can avoid mistakes in initiating the series of hajj and umrah worship.

### Why is Miqat Crucial?
Miqat is one of the provisions established by the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. If someone passes Miqat without the intention of ihram, they must return to Miqat to assume the intention. If it is not possible to return, there are legal consequences that must be met according to fiqh provisions.

Therefore, every pilgrim needs to know the Miqat location they will pass before departure.

### Five Makani Miqats
Here are the five main Miqats used by pilgrims based on their direction of arrival.

1. **Dhul Hulaifah (Abyar Ali)**
   The Miqat for pilgrims coming from Medina. This location is the most frequently used Miqat for Indonesian pilgrims who first visit Medina before going to Makkah.

2. **Al-Juhfah**
   Used by pilgrims coming from the Levant (Syria), Egypt, and West Africa. Currently, some pilgrims usually assume Ihram in Rabigh, which is located close to Al-Juhfah.

3. **Qarnul Manazil**
   The Miqat for pilgrims coming from the Najd region and its surroundings.

4. **Yalamlam**
   Used by pilgrims coming from the direction of Yemen. Many flights from Southeast Asia pass through the air corridor parallel to Yalamlam, so pilgrims usually start making intentions for ihram when the aircraft approaches the area as directed by the guide.

5. **Dhat 'Irq**
   The Miqat for pilgrims coming from the direction of Iraq and the northeast region of the Arabian Peninsula.

### Route of Indonesian Pilgrims
In general, there are two travel scenarios for Indonesian pilgrims.

* **First Wave**
  Indonesia → Medina → Dhul Hulaifah → Makkah
  On this trip, pilgrims assume Miqat at Dhul Hulaifah Mosque before departing for Makkah.

* **Second Wave**
  Indonesia → Jeddah → Makkah
  Because the aircraft passes the Miqat zone in the air, pilgrims usually have put on their ihram clothes since the embarkation or in the plane, then make the ihram intention when the pilot or guide announces that the plane is parallel with the Miqat area.
my_learning_wizard.lesson_1_3_content: Launch the immersive 360° tour below to orient yourself with the pathways of Masjidil Haram, locating key landmarks including the Kaaba, Safa, and Marwah hills.

my_learning_wizard.lesson_2_2_content: Step-by-step overview of camp structures and stoning schedules.

lesson_1_1_quiz.q: What is the recommended daily walking duration to build stamina before Hajj?

lesson_1_2_quiz.q: Which Miqat boundary is designated for pilgrims arriving from Medina?

lesson_1_3_quiz.q: What is the starting point of the Tawaf ritual circuit?

readiness.objective: Understand physical fitness standards and cardio stamina protocols for daily walking.
readiness.desc: Welcome to Physical Readiness. Here you will learn about the physical fitness required before departure for Hajj, including walking exercises, mandatory medical preparation, and tips to maintain stamina.

miqat.objective: Identify geographical boundary zones and the correct protocols to enter the state of Ihram.
miqat.desc: Welcome to Understanding Miqat Boundaries. This lesson guides you to understand the boundaries (Miqat Makani) where you must assume Ihram, Indonesian pilgrim arrival routes, and consequences of passing without intention.

ihram.objective: Understand the Ihram intention from Miqat and forbidden acts during Ihram.
ihram.desc: Welcome to Ihram (Intention). This lesson covers the initial steps of the Hajj pilgrimage, including pronouncing the Ihram intention, correct dress code for men and women, and prohibitions.

wukuf.objective: Understand the peak ritual of Hajj at Mount Arafat on the 9th of Dhul-Hijjah.
wukuf.desc: Welcome to Wukuf in Arafat. Learn the procedures for performing wukuf as the most important pillar of Hajj, including its timing, recommended prayers/zikr, and etiquette.

mabit_muzdalifah.objective: Understand how to perform overnight stay (mabit) in Muzdalifah and gather pebbles.
mabit_muzdalifah.desc: Welcome to Mabit in Muzdalifah. Here you will study the obligation of spending the night briefly in Muzdalifah after Arafat wukuf, and how to collect stones for stoning the jamrah.

jumrah_aqabah.objective: Understand stoning the Jamrah Aqabah on the 10th of Dhul-Hijjah.
jumrah_aqabah.desc: Welcome to Stoning Jamrah Aqabah. Learn the detailed procedures for stoning 7 pebbles at Jamrah Aqabah on the day of Eid al-Adha (10 Zulhijah) as an important part of Hajj duties.

tahalul.objective: Understand shaving/cutting hair (tahallul) to lift Ihram prohibitions.
tahalul.desc: Welcome to Tahallul. Learn the rules of shaving or cutting at least 3 strands of hair after completing parts of the pilgrimage, marking the end of Ihram restrictions (Tahallul Awal or Tahallul Akhir).

tawaf.objective: Understand the Tawaf Ifadah/Wada circuit of Kaaba for 7 rounds.
tawaf.desc: Welcome to Tawaf. Learn the critical pillar of circumambulating the Kaaba 7 times counter-clockwise at Masjidil Haram, including conditions of validity, prayers, and procedures.

sai.objective: Understand Sa'i walking/running between the hills of Safa and Marwah 7 times.
sai.desc: Welcome to Sa'i. Study the rules and steps of walking and running between Safa and Marwah hills 7 times as a pillar of Hajj.

mabit_mina.objective: Understand staying in Mina and stoning three jamarat during Tashreeq days.
mabit_mina.desc: Welcome to Mabit in Mina & Lempar Jumrah. Learn the obligation of staying overnight in Mina during the Tashreeq days (11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul-Hijjah) and stoning the three jamarat (Ula, Wusta, Aqabah) in order.

wada.objective: Understand Tawaf Wada as the final farewell circumambulation.
wada.desc: Welcome to Tawaf Wada (Farewell). Learn the rules of performing the farewell tawaf before leaving the holy city of Makkah, marking the end of your Hajj pilgrimage.
